items.title: Presentation: The Human Scalability Problem: Why Your Teams Don’t Scale Like Your Code
items.summaryShort: Presentation: The Human Scalability Problem: Why Your Teams Don’t Scale Like Your Code. Charlotte de Jong Schouwenburg discusses the "human bottlenecks" of hyper-growth.

items.title: Article: From Batch to Micro-Batch Streaming: Lessons Learned the Hard Way in a Delta Index Pipeline
items.summaryShort: Article: From Batch to Micro-Batch Streaming: Lessons Learned the Hard Way in a Delta Index Pipeline. This article describes how a production delta-index pipeline migrated from scheduled batch to micro-batch Spark Structured Streaming.

items.title: Podcast: Roq: Leveraging Quarkus to Build Static Sites at the Speed of Go
items.summaryShort: Podcast: Roq: Leveraging Quarkus to Build Static Sites at the Speed of Go. Andy Damevin, a developer who worked on Quarkus for almost a decade, talks about Roq.

items.title: DoorDash Used Copilot to Convert Its XCTest-Based iOS Test Suite to Swift Testing

items.summaryShort: Java News Roundup: OpenJDK JEPs, GlassFish, Spring AI, JReleaser, A2A Java SDK, Google ADK, Gradle. This week's Java roundup for April 27th, 2026, features news highlighting: OpenJDK JEPs for JDK 27; the fifth milestone release of Spring AI

items.title: Amazon opens up its global logistics network to all businesses
items.summaryShort: Amazon opens up its global logistics network to all businesses. The new service, called Amazon Supply Chain Services, pits the e-commerce directly against UPS and FedEx.

items.title: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
items.summaryShort: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. The BOGO offer is live.

items.title: Barocal can cool your food and drink by squeezing a hunk of plastic crystals
items.summaryShort: Barocal can cool your food and drink by squeezing a hunk of plastic crystals. Barocal might have discovered a cheap, nonpolluting material that could dethrone today's refrigeration technology.
